title: Quadrature rule optimization
summary: A compact five-node rule for estimating one-dimensional integrals, improved against a fixed public test suite. The report separates the acceptance score from direct residual errors so the gain can be inspected, replayed, and challenged.
problem_statement: Find a compact quadrature rule that reduces integration error across a fixed suite of analytic functions while keeping the rule inspectable, replayable, and stable under the unchanged evaluation contract.

evaluation_contract.summary: Candidates are scored on a frozen analytic integrand suite using a lower-is-better objective. The objective is the acceptance contract, while the report also exposes per-integrand errors as the more interpretable scientific readout.

website.card_summary: A five-node integration rule is compared against a fixed baseline, with both the acceptance score and direct residual errors exposed.

figure_captions.assets/objective-curve.svg: Best-so-far objective over the curated public trace. The acceptance score is lower-is-better and should be read as the contract used to retain candidates.
figure_captions.assets/candidate-trace.svg: Accepted quadrature rule: node positions, normalized weights, and residual error by representative integrand.

website.score_note: The objective is the evaluation contract used by the run, not a universal measure of integration quality. The scientific interpretation comes from reading it together with the residual errors and the replayable candidate.
